objective: Record completed PRD corrections for Gem Team agent routing requirements after user feedback, preserving real agent slugs, decisions, verification outcomes, and gotchas for future agents.

decisions.decision: Use docs/PRD.md as the PRD source of truth.
rationale: No YAML PRD was created because the existing repository PRD format is Markdown.

decisions.decision: Use real gem-* slugs for routable/configurable agent references in the PRD, not uppercase display titles.
rationale: User feedback stated the earlier PRD agent names were wrong.
Research confirmed real agent slugs from https://github.com/mubaidr/gem-team/tree/main/.apm/agents.

decisions.decision: Keep gem-orchestrator in the canonical agent list but exclude it from its own child/subagent routing list.
rationale: gem-orchestrator is the primary user-invoked routing agent.
The child/subagent routing list must contain only the other 15 real Gem Team agents.

decisions.decision: Do not hardcode litellm/xxx model defaults in the PRD.
rationale: Model default values must be configurable.
Default, recommended, and acceptance-criteria text must avoid fixed litellm/xxx values.
Model selection is expressed through model_profiles.* keys.

decisions.decision: Route through gem-orchestrator without a separate auto model or /auto command.
rationale: The user specified routing occurs when calling gem-orchestrator.
A dedicated auto model and slash command are out of scope.

decisions.decision: Exclude long-term routing strategy and opencode configuration changes from this project.
rationale: The user stated long-term plans are not part of this project.
Modifying opencode configuration, provider-side routers, LiteLLM router strategy, and opencode.json acceptance criteria are outside scope.

facts: User feedback stated the previous PRD used wrong agent names and required checking https://github.com/mubaidr/gem-team/tree/main/.apm/agents.

Research confirmed the real Gem Team agent slugs: gem-browser-tester, gem-code-simplifier, gem-critic, gem-debugger, gem-designer-mobile, gem-designer, gem-devops, gem-documentation-writer, gem-implementer-mobile, gem-implementer, gem-mobile-tester, gem-orchestrator, gem-planner, gem-researcher, gem-reviewer, gem-skill-creator.
docs/PRD.yaml was not found and was not forcibly created.
docs/PRD.md has been modified to use real gem-* slugs for canonical and routable agent references.
The prior uppercase accepted agent-name list is superseded and must not be used for routable/configurable identifiers.
Model defaults must be configurable and must not be hardcoded as litellm/xxx values.
Model selection is expressed through model_profiles.* keys.
Routing occurs when invoking gem-orchestrator; no /auto command or standalone auto model is in scope.
Prompts, instructions, and slash commands are not positive implementation requirements in this PRD.
Long-term LiteLLM/provider-side router/plugin/opencode global configuration modification content is out of scope.
Acceptance criteria verify the PRD document itself.

uppercase_accepted_agent_name_list.reason: User feedback and source research confirmed routable/configurable identifiers must be real gem-* slugs rather than uppercase display titles.

gotchas: Do not introduce docs/PRD.yaml unless explicitly requested or repository standards change.
Do not reuse the old uppercase accepted list as routable/configurable identifiers; it is superseded by real gem-* slugs.
SIMPLIFIER must be represented as gem-code-simplifier.
DOCUMENTATION must be represented as gem-documentation-writer.
gem-orchestrator may appear in the canonical agent list but must not appear in its own child/subagent routing list.
Do not re-add hardcoded litellm/xxx defaults, recommendations, or acceptance criteria.
Do not describe /auto, slash-command workflows, standalone auto models, prompts, or instructions except as explicitly rejected/non-goals.
Do not add long-term solution, LiteLLM/provider-side router/plugin, opencode global configuration edits, or opencode.json acceptance criteria to this PRD.

results: docs/PRD.md lines 36-51 use 16 real gem-* slugs.
docs/PRD.md line 104 routable child/subagent list contains 15 agents and excludes gem-orchestrator.
No old erroneous routing/configuration names are used as routable/configurable identifiers.
No hardcoded default model ID such as litellm/xxx remains.
Model selection is expressed through model_profiles.* keys.
No /auto command or standalone auto model remains.
No positive requirement to generate or maintain prompts, instructions, or slash commands remains.
No long-term LiteLLM/provider-side router/plugin/opencode global configuration modification content remains.
Acceptance criteria verify only the PRD document itself.

patterns.name: Markdown PRD source of truth
patterns.description: When docs/PRD.md exists and docs/PRD.yaml is absent, update the existing Markdown PRD instead of creating a YAML PRD unless instructed.

patterns.name: Gem Team slug-based routing identifiers
patterns.description: Use real gem-* slugs from the upstream .apm/agents directory for canonical, routable, and configurable agent references.

patterns.name: gem-orchestrator-driven routing
patterns.description: Agent model routing requirements are expressed around gem-orchestrator invocation, not a standalone auto model or slash command; gem-orchestrator routes only to the other 15 child agents.

facts.statement: The real canonical Gem Team agent slug set is gem-browser-tester, gem-code-simplifier, gem-critic, gem-debugger, gem-designer-mobile, gem-designer, gem-devops, gem-documentation-writer, gem-implementer-mobile, gem-implementer, gem-mobile-tester, gem-orchestrator, gem-planner, gem-researcher, gem-reviewer, gem-skill-creator.
The old uppercase accepted list is superseded and must not be reused as routable/configurable identifiers.
gem-orchestrator is the primary user-invoked routing agent and is excluded from its own child/subagent routing list.
Model defaults must be configurable and must not be hardcoded to litellm/xxx values.
Model selection is expressed through model_profiles.* keys.

gotchas: Old uppercase agent names can reappear during edits; verify that routable/configurable identifiers are gem-* slugs.
SIMPLIFIER maps to gem-code-simplifier, not gem-simplifier.
DOCUMENTATION maps to gem-documentation-writer, not gem-documentation.
gem-orchestrator must be excluded from its own child routing list even though it belongs in the canonical list.
Negative-scope mentions of prompts, instructions, and chat commands are allowed, but implementation or acceptance requirements for them are not.

failure_modes.scenario: PRD update accidentally preserves stale routing requirements.
symptoms: uppercase display titles appear as routable/configurable identifiers
SIMPLIFIER or DOCUMENTATION appears instead of gem-code-simplifier or gem-documentation-writer
gem-orchestrator appears in its own child routing list
litellm/xxx appears as a default or recommendation
failure_modes.mitigation: Re-run a PRD review for real gem-* slugs, child routing exclusions, banned model defaults, banned /auto references, and scope boundaries before marking the task complete.

decisions.decision: Use real gem-* slugs as PRD agent identifiers.
rationale: User feedback invalidated the uppercase accepted list.
Upstream .apm/agents research confirmed the real slugs.

decisions.decision: Keep PRD scope focused on current project routing behavior only.
rationale: User excluded opencode configuration modification work.

conventions: Use gem-* slugs exactly as specified in the real canonical list for routable/configurable agent references.
Represent model choices as model_profiles.* keys, not provider/model IDs.
Represent prompts/instructions/chat commands only as exclusions or non-goals in this PRD context.
